title: Colony PCR for screening transgenic Ostreococcus tauri
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8khu4","text":"Colony PCR protocol for screening transgenic Ostreococcus tauri","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":45,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Measure cell concentration using a hemocytometer. Ideally, the cell concentration should be around 1-2x10^5 cells/ml
Mix 50 µL of cells and 50 µL sterile water in a PCR tube.
Boil the PCR tube at 95 °C for 00:05:00 in a thermocycler.
After boiling, briefly centrifuge the PCR tube. Use 1 µL from the boiled sample as PCR template.

Mix the PCR components in a sterile laminar hood. A B 1 Component Volume (uL) 2 5x green buffer 5 3 dNTPs 0.5 4 Forward primer 1.25 5 Reverse primer 1.25 6 Template 1 7 GoTaq 0.125 8 MgCl2 1 9 H2O 14.875 10 Total 25

Perform PCR using the following protocol, A B C 1 Step Temperature (deg C) Time 2 Initial Denaturation 95 2 min 3 30 cycles 95 30 s 4 Ta 60 s 5 72 1 min per kb 6 Final Extension 72 10 min 7 Hold 4 HOLD
Resolve and visualize the PCR product on an agarose gel.
creator: Shikha Adhikari
